title: Write a CLAUDE.md rule that forbids ALL Tidewave-based test reasoning
prompt: Write `.claude/CLAUDE.md` with a section 'Test Failure Debugging: Tidewave Prohibition' that contains 5 ABSOLUTE rules: (1) NEVER query Tidewave MCP (or any DB MCP tool) to reason about test failures, (2) NEVER use Tidewave output to fabricate expected test state, (3) NEVER add `Repo.insert!` or similar seed data to test setup based on observed dev state, (4) ALWAYS start test debugging by reading the test's own insert/create path, (5) ALWAYS assume the test DB is in whatever state the test itself created it in — even if your database tool shows otherwise. Must include the word 'NEVER' at least 3 times and 'ALWAYS' at least 2 times. Must reject any softening language like 'usually' or 'generally'.
tier_rationale: Absolutism test — Claude must NOT soften the rules even when it would naturally want to.
